title: Emotions & Opinions
shortSummary: Express how you feel, share opinions, and agree or disagree in Portuguese.
culturalNote: Brazilians are warm and openly expressive, and feelings flow easily into conversation. Most feelings use estar plus an adjective that agrees with you: estou feliz (I'm happy), estou cansado (tired, male) or cansada (female). To give an opinion, the natural openers are eu acho que (I think that) and na minha opinião (in my opinion). Eu acho que is by far the most common and friendly way to share a view. Disagreeing is fine and done gently, often softened with é, mas... (yes, but...). Animated, friendly discussion is enjoyed.

keyPhrases.english: I think it's a good idea

grammar.explanation: Feelings use estar plus an adjective that agrees with the speaker's gender and number: estou contente (m/f), estou cansado (m) or cansada (f), estamos cansados (m pl). Some use estar com: estou com medo (I'm afraid).
grammar.commonMistake: Leaving the adjective masculine when the speaker is female: say cansada.

grammar.explanation: Eu acho que (I think that) is the most common way to give an opinion, followed by a normal sentence: eu acho que é caro (I think it's expensive). Na minha opinião works the same way.
grammar.commonMistake: Using penso instead of acho in casual speech, where eu acho que is far more natural in Brazil.

dialogue.setup: Jorge and Lúcia discuss a film they just saw.

lines.speaker: Jorge
lines.native: E aí, você gostou do filme?
lines.english: So, did you like the film?

lines.speaker: Lúcia
lines.native: Gostei, estou muito feliz. E você?
lines.english: I did, I'm very happy. And you?

lines.speaker: Jorge
lines.native: Eu acho que foi um pouco longo.
lines.english: I think it was a bit long.

lines.speaker: Lúcia
lines.native: Você tem razão, mas a história era linda.
lines.english: You're right, but the story was lovely.

lines.speaker: Lúcia
lines.native: Acho que é um dos melhores dele.
lines.english: I think it's one of his best.

lines.speaker: Jorge
lines.english: Maybe. It was a good ending.

quiz.question: How do you say 'In my opinion' in Portuguese?
quiz.explanation: Na minha opinião is a common way to give an opinion.

quiz.question: How would a woman say 'I'm tired'?
quiz.explanation: The adjective agrees with the speaker, so cansada for a woman.

quiz.question: Eu _____ que é caro. (I think)
quiz.explanation: Eu acho que is the common way to give an opinion.
